texto: St. Augustine says something which is a great thought and a great comfort here. He interprets the passage from the Psalms ‘seek his face always’ as saying: this applies ‘for ever’; to all eternity. God is so great that we never finish our searching. He is always new. With God there is perpetual, unending encounter, with new discoveries and new joy. Such things are theological matters. At the same time, in an entirely human perspective, I look forward to being reunited with my parents, my siblings, my friends, and I imagine it will be as lovely as it was at our family home.
explicacao: Citação de Peter Seewald sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “St. Augustine says something which is a great thought and a great comfort here. He interprets the…” articula amizade, eternity, heaven em registro de amizade, com relevância para contextos como termino e heaven.
